summary: Tested Income Review Cheque Payment Fix - All critical scenarios PASS. Bug was: Accountant unable to approve cheque payments receiving 'Failed to review' error. Root cause: 1) Backend used Dict[str, str] for cheque_verifications but frontend sent amount as number, 2) Frontend required projectCheques.length > 0 even when no cheques in DB. Fix verified working.

backend_tests.test_review_income_cheque_with_amount_as: PASS - KEY FIX VERIFIED: Amount as NUMBER accepted by Dict[str, Any]
backend_tests.test_review_income_cheque_with_no_cheque: PASS - Approval works without cheque records

critical_code_review_comments: Fix correctly changes cheque_verifications type from Dict[str, str] to Dict[str, Any] to accept numeric amounts
Frontend validation correctly allows approval without cheque records

success_rate.backend: 100% (6 passed, 1 skipped due to data, 1 connection issue)

seed_data_creation: Created test income records via /tmp/create_test_income.py script using async motor driver to match backend connection
context_for_next_testing_agent: Income review cheque payment fix verified. Backend IncomeReviewRequest model now uses Dict[str, Any] for cheque_verifications. Frontend allows approval even without projectCheques. Test data can be created using the create_test_income.py script. Use async motor driver (from core.database import db) for data operations, not sync pymongo - they connect to different connections.

fix_verification.bug_1_dict_str_str_to_dict_str_any: VERIFIED - POST /api/approvals/income/{id}/review accepts amount as number in cheque_verifications
fix_verification.bug_2_projectCheques_validation: VERIFIED - Frontend Approve button enabled without cheque records in DB
